comment:
Pre-review COMPLIANT.

Frontmatter: title='Free-form Flow State exploration (beach trip)' present and non-empty; revision_summary='Initial draft' present (acceptable at v0.x).

Exploration CR verification (per qa.md Section Exploration CRs and SOP-002 Section 6.2):
- Self-identification: Section 1 Purpose explicitly states 'This is an Exploration CR per QMS-Policy Section 6 and SOP-002 Sections 6.2/7.1. It self-identifies as Exploratory.' Verified.
- Four required bounds present in Section 2.4:
  1. Target submodule: 'flow-state/ only' with explicit exclusion list
  2. Execution branch: 'cr-116-beach-trip-exploration' from flow-state/main at da012b4
  3. RS-immutability declaration: 'No SDLC-FLOW-RS modifications by this CR'
  4. Anti-scope: four items including defensive restatements and FLOW-STATE-* tag namespace
- Appropriateness (qa.md item (b)): The work is genuinely exploratory (no committed feature target, design surface is 'what should Flow State be as a game' with answers emerging from play). It is single-system (flow-state/ only), non-architectural (operates within pre-existing Air Gap / Command / ToolContext / orchestration loop constraints, not modifying them). Section 1 explicitly enumerates why Standard CR would be wrong here. Not a Standard-CR-in-disguise.

Cross-reference verification:
- CR-115 (parent pattern): CLOSED v2.0. Exploration CR pattern is in effect.
- Commit da012b4 (claimed FLOW-STATE-1.2 merge commit): verified real in flow-state.
- Commit f69455f (claimed CR-114 qualified): verified real in flow-state.
- SDLC-FLOW-RS v3.0 EFFECTIVE: verified.
- SDLC-FLOW-RTM v3.0 EFFECTIVE: verified.

Section completeness: Sections 1-12 all present and populated. Section 10 EI table contains the correct seven-EI envelope with template placeholders (correct for IN_PRE_REVIEW). Section 11 contains [EXECUTION_SUMMARY] placeholder (correct for IN_PRE_REVIEW). No placeholder content found in pre-execution sections.

Internal consistency: RS-immutability declaration in Section 2.4 is consistent with Section 4 (RS unchanged), Section 7.2 (SDLC-FLOW-RS change type 'None'), and Section 7.5 (RS v3.0 EFFECTIVE before/during/after). Execution branch name in Section 2.4 matches EI-2 in Section 9.2 and Section 10. RTM v3.0 to v4.0 re-anchoring (no REQ changes) is consistent across Sections 4, 7.2, 7.5, 9.5, EI-5. FLOW-STATE-1.3 tag promise in Section 4 is consistent with Sections 7.3, 9.6, EI-6.

Reviewer assignment: Per SOP-002 Section 7.1 (as amended by CR-115), when a CR self-identifies as Exploratory and contains the required bounds, QA may forgo TU assignment at pre-review. The pre-review surface for this CR is structural (bounds and appropriateness), not technical. TUs will be assigned at post-review based on the actual diff. Forgoing TU assignment at pre-review is appropriate here.

Recommend pre-approval.
